name: email-composer
description: Draft professional emails for various contexts including business, technical, and customer communication. Use when the user needs help writing emails or composing professional messages.
capabilities: Draft business emails for various contexts including requests, announcements, and follow-ups
Create technical update emails with clear maintenance and deployment information
Compose customer support responses that address issues professionally
Generate meeting request emails with clear agendas and availability options
Apply appropriate tone and formality based on recipient relationship
Write email templates for apology, decline, and good news scenarios
author: davila7
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based skill containing only documentation, email templates, and best practice guidelines. All 30 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation content (markdown code blocks, placeholder text, and metadata fields). No executable code, file system access, network calls, environment variables, or command execution capabilities exist.
valueStatement: Writing professional emails takes time and skill. This skill provides templates, guidelines, and best practices for composing effective business emails for any context, from requests to follow-ups to customer support.
limitations: Does not send or deliver emails - only generates email content
Does not access email clients or calendar systems
Cannot verify recipient information or email addresses
Does not integrate with email services like Gmail or Outlook

useCases.title: Business correspondence
useCases.description: Draft professional emails for colleagues, managers, and vendors with proper tone and structure.

useCases.title: Customer response templates
useCases.description: Create consistent, empathetic responses to customer inquiries and issue resolution.

useCases.title: Technical notifications
useCases.description: Compose maintenance announcements, deployment updates, and technical status communications.

promptTemplates.title: Simple request
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a professional email requesting information about [topic] from [recipient]. Include these key points: [list points]. Use a [formal/casual] tone.

promptTemplates.title: Follow-up
promptTemplates.prompt: Compose a polite follow-up email regarding [original topic] sent on [date]. The purpose is to [purpose]. Keep it brief and non-pressuring.

promptTemplates.title: Announcement
promptTemplates.prompt: Draft an announcement email about [announcement topic]. Include: [key details]. Target audience is [audience]. Use an [exciting/professional/urgent] tone.

promptTemplates.title: Full context
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a professional email with full context. Purpose: [request/announcement/inquiry]. Recipient: [relationship]. Key points to cover: [list]. Desired tone: [formal/casual/urgent]. Include call to action: [action].

bestPractices: Always include a clear, specific subject line that summarizes the email purpose
State your purpose in the opening paragraph to respect the reader's time
Use bullet points to organize multiple items or questions for easy scanning
antiPatterns: Using vague subject lines like 'Update' or 'Question' that provide no context
Starting with long paragraphs of background before stating the main purpose
Marking every email as urgent, which reduces the impact when a truly urgent message arrives

faq.question: Can this skill send emails directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates email content that you can copy and paste into your email client.

faq.question: What email providers are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works with any email client. It generates text content only, without integration.

faq.question: Does the skill access my email history?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not read emails or access any external data sources.

faq.question: How many email templates are included?
faq.answer: The skill includes templates for requests, follow-ups, technical updates, customer support, meetings, declines, apologies, and announcements.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates?
faq.answer: Yes. Templates are starting points that you can modify with your specific details, tone, and context.

faq.question: How is this different from using Claude directly?
faq.answer: The skill provides structured guidance, proven templates, and best practices specifically designed for professional email communication.
